title: salix phylicifolia subsp. divaricata
intro: Salix phylicifolia subsp. divaricata, also known as the divergent willow, is a subspecies of the willow tree that is native to Ireland and other parts of Europe. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fever and rheumatism. The bark and leaves of the Salix phylicifolia subsp. divaricata contain salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which has been used to treat various health issues, including headaches, joint pain, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, providing relief from a range of health problems.
